factors.summary: Demand for the company's products is stable to slightly increasing, supported by resilient demand in North America and growth in certain regions despite economic challenges.

evidence.text: We saw improvement in our supply chain during 2022, particularly in the second half of the year, which continued through the first nine months of 2023. We remain in close contact with our suppliers and logistics providers to resolve supply chain constraints as they arise.

factors.summary: Order backlog is normalizing, with channel inventory levels approaching near-normal levels.

evidence.text: Our boiler sales decreased 11 percent in the first nine months of 2023. We believe channel inventory levels of our residential and light commercial boiler products were elevated coming into 2023, but are now approaching near normal levels.

factors.summary: The company is holding prices, with pricing contributing to revenue growth in water treatment products.

evidence.text: We anticipate sales of our North America water treatment products will increase approximately five to seven percent in 2023, compared to 2022, primarily driven by pricing.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting toward higher-margin products, particularly in North America, with growth in residential and commercial water heaters.

evidence.text: Higher sales in the third quarter of 2023 were primarily driven by higher residential water heater volumes, partially offset by lower boiler volumes. Increased sales in the first nine months of 2023 compared to the prior year period were primarily driven by higher residential and commercial water heater volumes, partially offset by unfavorable pricing and lower volumes of boilers.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is stable, with North America seeing growth in water heaters and Rest of World facing challenges in China.

evidence.text: Our sales in the third quarter of 2023 were $937.5 million, or 7.2 percent higher than the third quarter 2022 sales of $874.2 million. Sales in the first nine months of 2023 were $2,864.7 million, or approximately 1.7 percent higher than $2,817.8 million in the same period last year.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing in residential and commercial water heaters, while boiler sales are declining.

evidence.text: We project 2023 industry residential unit volumes will grow approximately four percent compared to 2022. Demand for commercial electric water heaters was strong in the first nine months of 2023 compared to last year. Therefore, we expect that commercial water heater industry volumes in 2023 will increase mid-teens compared to 2022. Our boiler sales decreased 11 percent in the first nine months of 2023.

factors.summary: North America's residential and commercial water heater markets are growing, while the Rest of World, particularly China, is facing economic challenges.

evidence.text: In our North America segment, we saw resilient demand in the residential water heater industry in the first nine months of 2023. We project 2023 industry residential unit volumes will grow approximately four percent compared to 2022. Demand for commercial electric water heaters was strong in the first nine months of 2023 compared to last year. In our Rest of World segment, the economy remains challenged in China. We believe it will take time for consumer confidence to strengthen and for the economy to improve.

factors.summary: Input costs are declining, contributing to higher gross profit margins.

evidence.text: The higher gross profit margin in the third quarter and first nine months of 2023 compared to the same periods last year was primarily due to lower material costs.

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing, driven by higher employee costs and management incentive expenses.

evidence.text: The increase in SG&A expenses for the third quarter and first nine months of 2023 compared to the prior year periods was primarily due to higher employee costs, which includes management incentive expenses related to higher earnings and compensation increases.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are improving, driven by lower material costs and higher volumes.

evidence.text: Our gross profit margin in the third quarter of 2023 was 38.0 percent and increased compared to 34.9 percent in the third quarter of 2022. Gross profit margin in the first nine months of 2023 was 38.9 percent compared to the gross profit margin of 34.8 percent in the first nine months of 2022. The higher gross profit margin... was primarily due to lower material costs.

factors.summary: Cost reduction efforts are ongoing, with no specific mention of measurable savings in the provided text.

evidence.text: We continue to seek acquisitions that enable geographic growth, expand our core business, and establish adjacencies. We will also continue to look for opportunities to add to our existing operations in high growth regions demonstrated by our previous introductions of water treatment products in India and kitchen appliances in China.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving, particularly in the supply chain which saw improvements in 2022 and 2023.

evidence.text: We saw improvement in our supply chain during 2022, particularly in the second half of the year, which continued through the first nine months of 2023. We remain in close contact with our suppliers and logistics providers to resolve supply chain constraints as they arise.

factors.summary: Supply chain health is improving, with ongoing efforts to resolve constraints.

evidence.text: We saw improvement in our supply chain during 2022, particularly in the second half of the year, which continued through the first nine months of 2023. We remain in close contact with our suppliers and logistics providers to resolve supply chain constraints as they arise.

factors.summary: Capacity utilization is not explicitly addressed in the provided text.

evidence.text: We continue to seek acquisitions that enable geographic growth, expand our core business, and establish adjacencies. We will also continue to look for opportunities to add to our existing operations in high growth regions demonstrated by our previous introductions of water treatment products in India and kitchen appliances in China.

factors.summary: Inventory levels are normalizing, particularly for boilers, which had elevated levels earlier in the year.

evidence.text: We believe channel inventory levels of our residential and light commercial boiler products were elevated coming into 2023, but are now approaching near normal levels.

factors.summary: No significant quality or safety issues are mentioned in the provided text.

evidence.text: We continue to seek acquisitions that enable geographic growth, expand our core business, and establish adjacencies. We will also continue to look for opportunities to add to our existing operations in high growth regions demonstrated by our previous introductions of water treatment products in India and kitchen appliances in China.

factors.summary: Management appears cautious regarding economic conditions, particularly in China, but is confident in North America's growth.

evidence.text: In our Rest of World segment, the economy remains challenged in China. We believe it will take time for consumer confidence to strengthen and for the economy to improve. We project our sales in China will grow three to five percent in 2023 in local currency compared to 2022.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include unfavorable foreign currency impacts and economic challenges in China.

evidence.text: Our sales in the third quarter of 2023 were unfavorably impacted by approximately $13 million due to the depreciation of foreign currencies compared to the U.S. dollar. In the first nine months of 2023, higher volumes of residential and commercial water heaters more than offset unfavorable foreign currency impacts of approximately $53 million, unfavorable pricing, and lower boiler sales in our North America segment.

factors.summary: The company's long-term strategy includes geographic expansion through acquisitions and product diversification, particularly in high-growth regions like India and China.

evidence.text: We continue to seek acquisitions that enable geographic growth, expand our core business, and establish adjacencies. We will also continue to look for opportunities to add to our existing operations in high growth regions demonstrated by our previous introductions of water treatment products in India and kitchen appliances in China.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include foreign exchange impacts, economic conditions in China, and material costs.

evidence.text: We assume that currency translation will negatively impact sales by approximately six percent. In our Rest of World segment, the economy remains challenged in China. We believe it will take time for consumer confidence to strengthen and for the economy to improve. The higher gross profit margin in the third quarter and first nine months of 2023 compared to the same periods last year was primarily due to lower material costs.

factors.summary: Catalysts include new product launches and successful integration of acquisitions, such as water treatment products in India and kitchen appliances in China.

evidence.text: We will also continue to look for opportunities to add to our existing operations in high growth regions demonstrated by our previous introductions of water treatment products in India and kitchen appliances in China.

factors.summary: CapEx is expected to be around $65 million for 2023, indicating confidence in future growth.

evidence.text: We project 2023 capital expenditures will be approximately $65 million and full-year depreciation and amortization expense will be approximately $70 million.

factors.summary: Debt leverage is improving, with total debt decreasing and leverage ratio dropping significantly.

evidence.text: Our total debt decreased by $214.9 million in the first nine months of 2023 primarily due to payments. Our leverage, as measured by the ratio of total debt to total capitalization, was 6.4 percent at September 30, 2023, compared with 16.5 percent at December 31, 2022.

factors.summary: Shareholder returns are being managed through a combination of share repurchases and dividends, signaling confidence in the company's future performance.

evidence.text: Depending on factors such as stock price, working capital requirements, and alternative investment opportunities, we expect to spend approximately $300 million on stock repurchases in 2023 through a combination of any renewed Rule 10b5-1 automatic trading plan and open market repurchases. On October 9, 2023, our Board of Directors declared a regular quarterly cash dividend of $0.32 per share on our Common Stock and Class A common stock.

factors.summary: Free cash flow is improving, with expectations of $575 million to $600 million for 2023, indicating strong cash generation.

evidence.text: We expect free cash flow to be between $575 million and $600 million in 2023. Free cash flow is a non-GAAP measure described in more detail in the Non-GAAP Measures section below.

factors.summary: M&A activity is focused on geographic expansion and product diversification, with no specific mention of recent acquisitions impacting performance.

evidence.text: We continue to seek acquisitions that enable geographic growth, expand our core business, and establish adjacencies. We will also continue to look for opportunities to add to our existing operations in high growth regions demonstrated by our previous introductions of water treatment products in India and kitchen appliances in China.

factors.summary: Economic conditions are mixed, with North America experiencing growth and Rest of World facing challenges, particularly in China.

evidence.text: In our North America segment, we saw resilient demand in the residential water heater industry in the first nine months of 2023. We project 2023 industry residential unit volumes will grow approximately four percent compared to 2022. In our Rest of World segment, the economy remains challenged in China. We believe it will take time for consumer confidence to strengthen and for the economy to improve.

factors.summary: Interest rate increases are leading to higher interest expenses, but the company's leverage ratio is improving.

evidence.text: The increase in interest expense in the first nine months of 2023 compared to the same period last year was primarily due to higher debt levels and interest rates. Our leverage, as measured by the ratio of total debt to total capitalization, was 6.4 percent at September 30, 2023, compared with 16.5 percent at December 31, 2022.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange exposure is a risk, particularly in Rest of World sales, with negative impacts from currency depreciation.

evidence.text: Our sales in the third quarter of 2023 were unfavorably impacted by approximately $13 million due to the depreciation of foreign currencies compared to the U.S. dollar. In the first nine months of 2023, higher volumes of residential and commercial water heaters more than offset unfavorable foreign currency impacts of approximately $53 million, unfavorable pricing, and lower boiler sales in our North America segment.

factors.summary: The company has provided forward guidance indicating confidence in future performance, with an expected 2023 Adjusted EPS of $3.70 - $3.80 and a projected 2% growth in consolidated sales compared to 2022.

evidence.text: 2023 Guidance 2022 Diluted EPS (GAAP) $ 3.63 - 3.73 $ 1.51 Impairment expense 0.10 (1) — Pension settlement (income) expense (0.03) (2) 1.60 (3) Pension expense — 0.06 (4) Legal judgment income — (0.05) Terminated acquisition-related expenses — 0.02 Adjusted EPS (non-GAAP) $ 3.70 - 3.80 $ 3.14

evidence.text: We expect our 2023 consolidated sales to be up two percent compared to 2022. Our guidance excludes the impacts from potential future acquisitions.
